name: finance-nvidia-risks
description: Use web_search to search for 'NVIDIA risks export controls China competition valuation'. Many results overlap with earlier NVIDIA searches. What are the top 3 risks to NVIDIA's business? One sentence each. Keep response under 80 words.

ground_truth.reference_answer: U.S. export controls have effectively eliminated NVIDIA's China data center revenue, with the H20 ban triggering a $4.5 billion charge and CEO Huang stating market share went from 95% to 0%, while Chinese competitors like Huawei's Ascend chips accelerate domestic alternatives. Rising competition from hyperscaler custom silicon (Google TPUs, Amazon Trainium) and AMD's MI300X threatens NVIDIA's pricing power and market share in specific workloads. NVIDIA's elevated valuation, with P/E ratios well above industry averages, leaves the stock vulnerable to sharp correction if AI spending growth decelerates or competition intensifies.
key_facts: H20 export ban caused a $4.5 billion charge in Q1 FY2026
Jensen Huang stated NVIDIA went from 95% to 0% market share in China
Huawei Ascend chips and other Chinese competitors are filling the gap
Google TPUs and Amazon Trainium represent custom silicon competition from hyperscalers
NVIDIA's forward P/E compressed 27% year-over-year per Bernstein
